luna-low.description: Cheapest reasoning-capable configuration. Fast and economical, but unreliable on long-horizon implementation, difficult debugging, and recovery after a wrong approach.

luna-medium.description: Large capability improvement over Luna low at modest cost. Reasonable repository understanding and scoped coding ability, but still inconsistent on autonomous multi-step implementation.

luna-high.description: Strong coding performance for the price. Capable implementation and terminal use, but relatively token-hungry, slower, and more likely to meander than stronger base models at comparable quality.

luna-max.description: Meaningfully stronger than Luna high and competitive with more expensive models. Excellent monetary efficiency, but very high token consumption, increased latency, and less concise execution.

terra-high.description: Best normal Terra configuration. Strong, balanced implementation, terminal execution, and repository understanding with better efficiency and discipline than compute-heavy Luna configurations.

terra-max.description: Material capability improvement over Terra high, especially for difficult implementation and persistent tool-driven work. Substantially more expensive, but the additional reasoning produces measurable gains.

sol-medium.description: Sol's practical efficiency sweet spot. Strong judgment, ambiguity handling, debugging, and cross-cutting reasoning while retaining much lower latency and token usage than higher Sol efforts.

sol-high.description: Near-maximum practical Sol capability. Strong across implementation, tools, architecture, and difficult reasoning, but only moderately better than Sol medium. Higher Sol reasoning levels usually add disproportionate cost and latency.
